response:
<<if statement>> <<to switch>> <<control flow>> <<variant>> <<is required>> <<for the>> <<given code>> <<structure>> <<to be>> <<converted>> <<with optimal>> <<efficiency>> <<ensured>> <<in the>> <<resulting>> <<function>>. <<The original>> <<if statement>> <<evaluates the>> <<grade variable>> <<and assigns>> <<a corresponding>> <<gradeValue>> <<based on>> <<the grade>> <<letter>>. <<A switch>> <<statement can>> <<achieve the>> <<same result>> <<by using>> <<case labels>> <<for each>> <<possible grade>> <<value>>.

<<Here is>> <<the equivalent>> <<switch statement>> <<that replaces>> <<the if-else>> <<chain with>> <<a switch>> <<construct>>:

```javascript
switch (grade) {
    case "A":
        gradeValue = 4.0;
        break;
    case "B":
        gradeValue = 3.0;
        break;
    case "C":
        gradeValue = 2.0;
        break;
    case "D":
        gradeValue = 1.0;
        break;
}
```

<<This switch>> <<statement is>> <<more efficient>> <<than the>> <<original if-else>> <<chain because>> <<it uses>> <<a lookup>> <<table mechanism>> <<under the>> <<hood>> <<which can>> <<be faster>> <<for multiple>> <<discrete conditions>>. <<Each case>> <<is checked>> <<in sequence>> <<until a>> <<match is>> <<found and>> <<the corresponding>> <<code is>> <<executed>>. <<This avoids>> <<the need>> <<for repeated>> <<equality checks>> <<as in the>> <<if-else structure>>. <<Additionally>> <<the switch>> <<statement is>> <<easier to>> <<read and>> <<maintain when>> <<multiple conditions>> <<are involved>>.
